sections.paragraph:
περιβόητος, ον (βοάω; Soph., Thu. et al.) pert. to being acclaimed far and wide, well known, far famed, celebrated (so Demosth. 34, 29; Menand., Fgm. 402, 3 Kö.; Plut., Ages. 609c [24, 5], Themist. 119 [15, 4]; 2 Macc 2:22; Philo, Mos. 2, 284 εὐσέβεια; Jos., Ant. 6, 165, C. Ap. 1, 315) φιλαδελφία 1 Cl 47:5; (w. σεμνός and πᾶσιν ἀνθρώποις ἀξιαγάπητος) ὄνομα 1:1.—DELG s.v. βοή.

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Themist Themist , IV a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 799
